name: Mothership Radial Disc
description: Massive radial disc craft, typically hundreds of meters to miles across. Surface is a dense fractal greeble layer with thousands of point-lights punched through. The Trumbull aesthetic: luminescence overwhelms hardware. The craft reads as a city of lights rather than a vehicle. Bloom should override silhouette. CE3K's mothership was an inverted city of light drawn from a memory of a night oil refinery.

ir_signature.ir_notes: Pre-IR era archetype. Not designed for sensor footage context. Optical/photographic context only.

cultural_context: The Trumbull/Spielberg Close Encounters mothership (1977) is the canonical reference: a 300–450 meter inverted city filmed in smoke-filled rooms on 65mm with thousands of neon pin-lights. Also ID4's 15-mile City Destroyer (concave, petal bas-relief) and V's 3–5 mile motherships. The mothership class establishes luminescence-as-hull and spectral-light-as-language.
